verdicts.reason: Faithful adapted-port: core fix lists custom themes by content name via loadThemeFromPath().name; dedup/precedence/sort match upstream; built-in path:undefined adaptation matches otto convention; regression test pins it.

Only the two target files touched (theme.ts + co-located new test). All edits confined to the three theme-listing functions serving the content-name fix; dedup refactor is upstream-justified collateral. No scope creep or out-of-target edits.

Test writes foo.json with name:bar, asserts bar listed/foo absent + path mapping. Genuinely fails pre-fix (slice file-stem) passes post-fix. Real fs/env/loadThemeFromPath, not mocked. 2nd test pins path:undefined convention.

Picker now lists custom themes by content name, but setTheme/loadThemeJson still resolves customThemesDir/.json by name. A foo.json named 'bar' becomes unselectable (throws→silent fallback to dark)—regresses the very case it fixes. Resolver not updated.
